name: Redd-Archiver
what_it_does: PostgreSQL-backed tool that transforms compressed Reddit/Voat/Ruqqus data dumps into searchable HTML archives with full-text search, REST API, and an MCP server with 29 tools for AI assistant integration.
use_cases: Create offline searchable archives of subreddits from Pushshift data dumps
Academic research on online discourse patterns and community dynamics
Community preservation before subreddit bans or platform shutdowns
Analysis of deleted or removed content from archived dumps
AI-assisted research over large social media corpora via MCP tools
not_for: Real-time Reddit browsing or live data access
Small datasets — optimized for multi-billion post corpora
best_when: You have Pushshift or equivalent data dumps and need a full-featured, searchable offline archive with AI assistant access via MCP — especially for research or preservation purposes.
avoid_when: You need live Reddit data access; use Reddit's API or an MCP server like reddit-mcp-buddy instead.

auth.notes: DATABASE_URL environment variable for PostgreSQL connection. Documentation notes default passwords should be changed. No API auth layer documented.

pricing.notes: Released under Unlicense (public domain). Infrastructure costs for PostgreSQL storage can be significant given dataset sizes (billions of posts).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Limited documentation on error handling; no structured error response schema documented
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Archive generation is a read-heavy pipeline; MCP tools are read-only queries against the archive
known_agent_gotchas: Requires downloading and importing multi-hundred-GB Pushshift dumps before any queries work
PostgreSQL GIN index creation on large datasets is time-consuming (hours to days)
Default database passwords need manual changing — security risk if not addressed
29 MCP tools may create tool-selection confusion for agents without good descriptions
Tor-optimized design means some features may behave differently on clearnet deployments

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
